title: Kiper's new two-round NFL mock draft: 64 picks, 64 landing spots for top prospects
content: Mel Kiper Jr. predicts 64 picks across the first two rounds of the draft, including two potential trades, a few QB landing spots and plenty of impact additions.

title: Spo: Ball should've been tossed for Adebayo trip
content: Heat center Bam Adebayo suffered a lower back injury after a fall in the second quarter and was ruled out in the second half.

title: Blazers ride Avdija's 41 over Suns, grab 7th seed
content: Deni Avdija scored 41 points to help the Blazers erase an 11-point deficit in the fourth quarter for a 114-110 win over the Suns that  secured the No. 7 seed in the Western Conference playoffs.

title: Sources: Giants' talks with Lawrence hit impasse
content: The Giants and star defensive tackle Dexter Lawrence II have reached an impasse on talks over a new contract extension, sources told NFL Network on Tuesday night.

title: Duran: Obscene gesture due to fan crossing line
content: Red Sox outfielder Jarren Duran directed an obscene gesture toward a fan who he said told him to "kill myself" during Boston's loss to the Twins on Tuesday.

title: Full plate: Lehigh scores 20 runs in first inning
content: Thirty-two Lehigh players batted during a 20-run first inning that set an NCAA record during a 38-6 blowout win over Coppin State.

title: Sources: Ekitike ruptures Achilles, misses WC
content: Hugo Ekitike will miss the World Cup and be out for at least nine months after he ruptured his Achilles tendon on Tuesday night, a source told ESPN.

title: Jackets coach torches players: 'They don't care'
content: Blue Jackets coach Rick Bowness questioned his players' desire to win after an uninspiring season-ending loss to the Capitals.

title: Undecided Ovi hears chants of 'One more year!'
content: Alex Ovechkin, who assisted on Jakob Chychrun's go-ahead goal during the third period of the Washington Capitals' 2-1 victory over the Columbus Blue Jackets in the season finale Tuesday, heard chants of "One more year!" from fans during the final minute.

content: Legwold and the late McGinnis formed a bond over combine breakfasts, large trucks, makeshift wallets and bobbleheads.

content: Tyson Fury's victory in his boxing return wasn't enough to re-enter the top 10. Here are the latest ESPN pound-for-pound rankings.

content: Alleging violation of legal procedures in the detention, the party leaders accuses State government of misusing police machinery for political vendetta

content: Two senior leaders from the JD(U), Bijendra Prasad Yadav and Vijay Kumar Choudhary, also took oath as Ministers

title: Telangana set to gain 60 more Assembly seats in delimitation
content: Hyderabad, Medchal–Malkajgiri and Ranga Reddy districts will see their combined Assembly strength rise from 29 to 45

title: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu sets deadline for key irrigation projects
content: The Chief Minister stresses on structured strategy to harness the Godavari water with focus on long-term water security rather than ad hoc utilisation

title: Assembly Elections 2026 highlights: PM Modi holds roadshow in Tamil Nadu, says DMK losing 'for sure'
content: People showered flower petals on the PM Modi, who embarked on the near 1.5 km roadshow from Veppamoodu junction to Vadasery in this town, during the near one-hour event.

title: Subhas Chandra Bose: the paradox of a revolutionary’s theory and praxis
content: The leader synthesised Indian spirituality with Hegelian dialectics, evolving from an absolute idealist into a pragmatic revolutionary seeking to build a modern, socialist India — an analysis on Bose’s journey through philosophy, politics, and his plans for society and national regeneration

title: Subhas Chandra Bose: Philosophy, Politics, and Praxis - Part 2
content: Bose’s legacy is a synthesis of contradictions: a mystic who embraced science, a democrat who did not shy away from authoritarianism, a patriot who looked toward unified humanity; his egalitarian ideal remains a powerful counter-narrative to fissiparous tendencies in an independent India

title: Auto LPG shortage returns; drivers face long queues across Bengaluru
content: Drivers say the situation had improved slightly following recent protests, but has deteriorated again over the past two days

title: Yathindra draws line between legacy politics and JD(S) ‘family politics’
content: He says his father did not bring him into politics for ‘collection’ of bribes as alleged by Union Minister for Heavy Industries and Steel H.D. Kumaraswamy, but to assist him in politics after his elder brother died

title: "No One Can Stop Babri Masjid In Bengal": Humayun Kabir To NDTV
content: On several parties competing for Muslim votes, the Aam Janata Unnayan Party (AJUP) founder asked why they are not trying to beat the BJP by trying to get Hindus to vote for them.

title: Massive Fire Rips Through Lucknow Slum Cluster, Thick Smoke Seen Miles Away
content: A massive fire broke out at a slum cluster in Lucknow's Vikas Nagar area on Wednesday evening, destroying more than two dozen huts and triggering panic among residents.

title: 'Against Centre's Approach, Not Women's Quota Bill': M Kharge After Key Meet
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge said: "We are in support of the Women's Reservation Bill but have objection to the government's approach".

title: "Made To Work Alone On Rooftop, Phone Snatched": TCS Nashik Employee To NDTV
content: The woman, who has worked at TCS for six years, spoke on a video call to NDTV. She said she was transferred to the Nashik office and then made to work alone on a terrace separate from the main building.

title: Bengaluru Man Pushes Paralysed Mother To Death From 4th Floor
content: A 42-year-old man allegedly killed his elderly mother by pushing her from the fourth floor of their residence inBengaluru's RR Nagar.

title: Money Row Between 2 Friends Uncovers Amaravati Minors' Abuse: Sources
content: As the videos began circulating, a few locals got the whiff of it and began demanding investigation and strong action against those involved.

title: Trump Says Will "Fire" Federal Reserve Chair If He Stays Beyond Mandate
content: President Donald Trump renewed pressure Wednesday on Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, with the US leader threatening to fire the central bank chief if he stays beyond his mandate.

title: Indian LPG Vessel Reaches Gujarat After Crossing Strait Of Hormuz
content: It was the ninth Indian vessel to exit the Persian Gulf since early March, while about 15 other Indian-flagged ships remain in the region, awaiting passage, government officials had said earlier.

title: French Man Arrested For Fake Doctor's Notes Scam, Earned Over $1 Million
content: In France, a doctor's sick note allows an employee to stay away from work with the government picking up part of the salary.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US Denies Agreeing To Extend Iran Ceasefire: Report
content: US-Iran War Live: The Iran war began when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28. Iran responded with its own strikes on Israel and Gulf states that host US bases.

title: MK Stalin Calls For Black Flag Protest Across Tamil Nadu Over Delimitation
content: Stalin has urged every household in Tamil Nadu to participate in the protest, framing the delimitation issue as a fight to protect the state's political rights.

title: Video: Omar Abdullah Refuses To Cut 'Tricolour' Ribbon, Earns Praise
content: Chief Minister Omar Abdullah is seen walking towards the ribbon stretched across and then stopping short as he notices the national flag colours, shows a video.

content: The fresh bout of negotiations comes amid evolving tariff rules in the US, with officials indicating that clarity on Washington's trade stance remains crucial before any final deal.

content: Iran's military has also threatened to block trade through the Red Sea, along with the Gulf and Sea of Oman, if the US naval blockade continues.

content: Bengal with its duality - 38% women in Parliament versus 18% in the state contest - captures the core tension of the Bill better than any other example.

content: The self-enumeration exercise to continue until April 30 while house-listing and housing census will be conducted from May 1 to 30

content: Airlines are grappling with multiple challenges, including higher ATF costs, flight cancellations for Gulf destinations, war-risk premiums and longer routing for west-bound flights

content: The nine resolutions are aimed at strengthening social responsibility, health, sustainability and self-sufficiency

title: Udhayanidhi Stalin’s assets: Madras High Court directs Director General of Income Tax to respond by April 20
content: Chief Justice’s Bench also orders notices to the Deputy Chief Minister, Red Giant Movies, Snow Housing and Union Ministry of Corporate Affairs

title: Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni and 16 others convicted for 2016 murder of BJP leader in Dharwad
content: While the MLA has been convicted for both criminal conspiracy and murder under provisions of the Indian Penal Code (IPC), he and Chandrashekar Indi were acquitted of the charges under the Arms Act, 1959, as the sanction for their prosecution under this law was not obtained

title: Jagan’s ‘warning on privatisation of harbours’ is ridiculous, says Minister
content: Juvvaladinne harbour was inaugurated during the YSRCP tenure even before it was completed, says Anam Ramanarayana Reddy

title: 'Left lunatics might not like this': Trump shares AI image of Jesus hugging him
content: Former US President Donald Trump shared a new AI-generated image showing Jesus embracing him. This follows criticism for a previous image where he was depicted as Jesus healing a man. Trump defended the earlier post, claiming it was meant to represent him as a doctor. The controversial images have drawn varied reactions from different groups.

title: New 'Choudhary' of Magadh: Samarat's 'muretha' vow comes full circle with Nitish's exit
content: Samrat  Choudhary’s journey to the top has hardly followed a conventional BJP path. Unlike many leaders nurtured within the Sangh ecosystem, he comes from outside that fold. His father, veteran politician Shakuni Choudhary, was a founding member of the Samata Party and a contemporary of both Nitish Kumar and Lalu Prasad Yadav. Samrat himself began in the Rashtriya Janata Dal in the 1990s and entered government early, serving as a minister under Rabri Devi.

title: Snap to lay off 1,000 employees, CEO Evan Spiegel's memo puts it on AI
content: Snapchat's parent company is laying off 16% of its global workforce, impacting around 1,000 employees, as it pivots towards profitable growth and integrates AI to enhance efficiency. CEO Evan Spiegel announced the move, aiming to slash costs by over $500 million by 2026 to achieve net-income profitability.

title: After three dots, Rishabh Pant walks off — what happened?
content: Skipper Rishabh Pant was forced to leave the field after a painful elbow blow during Lucknow Super Giants’ match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Wednesday. Struggling against Josh Hazlewood, Pant was hit by a short ball and walked off in pain, with Nicholas Pooran replacing him, disrupting LSG’s early momentum.

title: Mixed signals: Trump says Iran war ending 'very soon' but 10K more troops head to Middle East
content: Over 10,000 additional US troops are deploying to the Middle East this month, including personnel aboard the USS George HW Bush and the Boxer Amphibious Ready Group. This build-up coincides with the potential end of a two-week ceasefire with Iran, as planning for ground operations, including securing enriched uranium, is under way.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 28: Ranveer Singh film marches toward Rs. 1100 crore milestone
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is redefining box office history with every passing day. Directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, the espionage thriller has become the first Hindi film ever to cross Rs. 1,000 crore in India net collections. On day 28, the film is chasing the Rs. 1,100 crore domestic milestone with a live India net of Rs. 1,096.19 crore and a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,727.93 crore.

title: 'Xi will give me big, fat hug': Trump says he opened Hormuz for China ... 'and also the world'
content: President Trump claims China is pleased with his decision to permanently open the Strait of Hormuz, stating he's acting for their benefit and the world's. He asserts China has agreed to cease weapon shipments to Iran and anticipates a warm reception from President Xi during an upcoming visit, highlighting smart cooperation over conflict.

title: 'Hissa chori': Rahul accuses Centre of 'power grab' using delimitation
content: Rahul Gandhi accused the Centre of a power grab through a delimitation bill, stating the women's reservation bill was already passed in 2023. He asserted the Congress party unequivocally supports women's reservation and condemned the proposed increase in Lok Sabha seats as 'hissa chori' from marginalized communities and certain regions.

title: Vance lauds his Indian-origin in-laws, H1-B workers; stresses prioritising American identity first
content: US Vice President JD Vance emphasized that while immigrants enrich America, new citizens must prioritize their American identity. Speaking at a Turning Point USA event, Vance acknowledged the economic contributions of H-1B visa holders, including his Indian-origin in-laws, but warned against fraud and misuse.

title: 'Only after hitting 6s against India ...': Pak's Farhan makes big revelation
content: Sahibzada Farhan said his favourite sixes came against India in the Asia Cup, where he hit three off Jasprit Bumrah. Despite his effort, India, led by Suryakumar Yadav and finished by Tilak Varma, won. Farhan credited confidence from attacking top bowlers but faced backlash for a controversial celebration, despite strong recent form.

title: WWE WrestleMania 42 Night 1 and 2 matches, main event, full predictions and more
content: WrestleMania 42 is set for Las Vegas, featuring a two-night spectacle hosted by John Cena. The event boasts marquee matchups including Cody Rhodes vs. Randy Orton for the Undisputed WWE Championship and CM Punk vs. Roman Reigns for the World Heavyweight Championship, with over 40,000 fans expected.

title: 'Ghost' army ready: How Australia aims to tip the balance in Indo-Pacific
content: Australia is rapidly advancing its autonomous warfare capabilities with the Ghost Shark underwater drone and Ghost Bat aerial drone. These homegrown programs, moving from prototype to production, aim to bolster regional strategic balance and reduce risks to human personnel in high-threat environments.

title: R Madhavan warns son Vedaant about fame, discipline, and public image
content: R Madhavan's success in 'Dhurandhar 2' is matched by his son Vedaant's swimming achievements. Madhavan has instilled values in Vedaant, emphasizing the responsibilities that come with public recognition. He expressed pride in Vedaant, acknowledging his son's privileged life while preparing him for public scrutiny.

title: Will Rohit play next IPL game? Mumbai Indians share injury update
content: Rohit Sharma is set to miss Mumbai Indians’ IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings due to a recurring hamstring injury sustained against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. MI said he is being assessed. Rohit retired hurt in that match, and his absence adds to MI’s struggles after three straight defeats this season.

title: Pakistan plans 2-hour daily power cuts as Iran war drives up energy costs
content: Pakistan is implementing two-hour evening power cuts due to soaring energy costs and Iran war-related disruptions. Reduced hydropower and reliance on expensive fossil fuels are straining the nation's finances. While urban areas face shorter outages, rural regions, particularly under MEPCO, are experiencing severe, prolonged blackouts, with gas supply to power plants also significantly impacted.

title: Virat Kohli not in XI, star RCB pacer returns against LSG
content: RCB batter Virat Kohli was left out of RCB’s starting XI against Lucknow Super Giants due to an ankle injury but remained available as an Impact Player. Captain Rajat Patidar chose to bowl first, while Rishabh Pant stayed confident. RCB brought in Josh Hazlewood as both teams looked to gain momentum.

title: Bombay HC likely to give protection of personality rights to Kartik Aaryan
content: Bombay High Court indicated it would direct the removal of online content violating Kartik Aaryan's personality rights. The court will issue an order to address unauthorized commercial use of his name and image, including AI-generated videos and merchandise sales. This follows Aaryan's plea to protect his publicity and privacy rights.

title: ‘I might not be here’: Ben Stokes ‘lucky to be alive’ after freak injury scare
content: Ben Stokes described a training incident that broke his cheekbone as a "pretty nasty" but ultimately fortunate event, stating he feels "quite lucky" to be alive. The injury delayed his return to cricket, forcing a reassessment of his fitness plan. Stokes also dismissed speculation about his working relationship with Brendon McCullum, emphasising their shared goal for the team.

title: Turkey school shooting: 9 killed, many hurt; 2nd such incident in two days
content: Tragedy struck Kahramanmaras, Turkey, as a middle school student unleashed gunfire, claiming nine lives including a teacher. The assailant, who used his father's hidden weapons, also perished. This grim event follows a similar shooting in Urfa the previous day, where a former student injured sixteen before taking his own life. Authorities are investigating both incidents.

title: ‘Sindarov got lucky’: German No. 1 Keymer on ‘critical moment' | Exclusive
content: Exclusive: Javokhir Sindarov won the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament, earning a World Championship match against D Gukesh. Germany No. 1 Vincent Keymer praised his aggressive and fearless style of play. "It was easy for him to lose the first game," said Keymer as Uzbek prodigy sealed the title with a round to spare.

title: CBSE class 10 result 2026: Region-wise pass percentage out; Trivandrum, Vijaywada top the list
content: CBSE Class 10 results for 2026 are out. Trivandrum and Vijayawada achieved the highest pass rates. Southern regions showed strong performance. Delhi regions also performed well. Guwahati recorded the lowest pass percentage. The overall pass percentage saw a slight increase. Results indicate stable performance nationwide with minor regional variations.

title: Subtle signs of organ stress most people ignore and how to prevent damage
content: Your body whispers warnings before it screams in pain. Subtle signs like persistent fatigue, brain fog, skin changes, and minor discomforts often signal internal strain. Experts urge paying attention to these early indicators, as they can prevent serious organ damage. Lifestyle adjustments and regular check-ups are key to proactive health.

title: ‘End of an Era’: Viktor Axelsen retires at 32, Lakshya Sen pays emotional tribute
content: Denmark's two-time Olympic champion Viktor Axelsen, 32, has retired due to persistent back issues, ending a dominant era in badminton. His career, marked by two Olympic golds and two World Championship titles, redefined the sport's physical standards. Axelsen's influence extended globally, particularly in China, where he connected with fans in Mandarin.

title: First time in 7 yrs! India gets 4m barrels of crude from Iran just ahead of Trump waiver expiry
content: The very large crude carrier Jaya, fully loaded with Iranian oil, is currently unloading its cargo at Paradip on India’s eastern coast. India is looking to quickly secure supplies ahead of a deadline set by the Donald Trump administration that expires over the weekend.

title: NCA feels more like a 'Hospital'... players are fearful of going: Munaf Patel
content: Former India pacer Munaf Patel has voiced strong concerns about India's cricketing ecosystem, urging reforms at the National Cricket Academy (NCA) and domestic structure. He criticized the NCA's focus on rehabilitation over holistic development and highlighted the lack of a strong feeder pathway, advocating for strengthened grassroots infrastructure.

title: CBSE Class 10th result 2026 declared: 93.70% students pass; southern states dominate
content: The CBSE has declared the Class 10 board results for 2026 on April 15, recording a pass percentage of 93.70%, a slight rise from last year. Over 24.7 lakh students appeared across India and abroad. Girls again outperformed boys, while southern regions and Kendriya Vidyalayas led institutional performance in a largely stable academic outcome.

title: Z security & ED raids: AAP-Raghav Chadha faceoff turns ugly; BJP piles on
content: The Aam Aadmi Party's rift with MP Raghav Chadha has widened, with leaders accusing him of betrayal and linking ED raids on Ashok Mittal to his alleged collusion with the BJP. Chadha's removal as deputy leader and subsequent Z-category security have fueled internal dissent and public accusations.

title: Meet Yvon Chouinard: The billionaire who gave away his $3 billion company to save the planet
content: Patagonia founder Yvon Chouinard made a groundbreaking move, gifting his $3 billion company to environmental causes.  Instead of personal profit, the company's earnings now fund climate action and conservation. This unique structure, separating control from financial benefit, ensures Patagonia's profits directly support the planet's health, sparking global debate on purpose-driven business.

title: Court asks filmmaker Rajiv Rai to refrain from talking about 'Dhurandhar 2'
content: Filmmaker Rajeev Rai has assured the Delhi High Court he will not speak to the media about the 'Dhurandhar 2' song dispute during ongoing mediation. Trimurti Films alleges unauthorized use of their song. The court emphasized restraint to aid mediation, noting Rai's public remarks were causing prejudice.

title: 'Very serious': UN watchdog flags sharp rise in North Korea's nuclear activity
content: North Korea's nuclear weapons production capability is seeing a "very serious increase," according to the IAEA chief. Heightened activity at the Yongbyon complex, including its reactor and reprocessing unit, signals a significant expansion. New uranium enrichment facilities are also under construction, boosting production capacity. This development raises global concerns about regional security and nuclear proliferation.

title: Stalin vs Centre: Why is Tamil Nadu protesting the delimitation bill
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin announced a state-wide black flag protest against the Centre's proposed delimitation exercise. He warned of severe political consequences if concerns about reducing southern states' political weight are ignored. Stalin described the move as a 'grave danger' and a 'historic injustice' that could widen the North-South divide and undermine federalism.

title: Drama in PSL: Sikandar Raza fires back at Imad Wasim over 'money' remark
content: The comments date back to last year, when Imad spoke about Raza’s late arrival for the PSL 10 final at Gaddafi Stadium in Lahore. Raza had taken a partial leave from the Qalandars squad to play a one-off Test for Zimbabwe against England in Nottingham.

title: Keys to the offseason: Free agency and draft plans for every eliminated NHL team
content: the 2026 Stanley Cup playoffs are about to begin, meaning 16 teams are looking forward to their shot at the best trophy in sports . how should they modify their rosters this summer via trades, free agency and the draft? read on for full breakdown on every team that is no longer eligible for the Stanley Cup in 2026 .

title: WNBA draft reaction: Breaking down best picks, shocking trade and more
content: the top four picks sorted themselves out a few days before the draft . Lauren Betts was selected at no. 4 by the Washington Mystics in the 2026 WNBA . the first round of the draft had 13 of the 15 players taken in the second and third rounds .

title: Predicting the entire postseason -- with a surprise champion

title: Men's transfer buzz: How Final Four teams are reloading

title: The college football fan's guide to this year's NFL draft: Top schools, trends, more
content: the Jets trade up in the first round to select Ohio State's Carnell Tate . the picks start coming off the board April 23 . you would have to go back to 1967 to find the last time a college program had four players selected in the top 10 picks (Michigan State)

title: Barnwell: Why four teams should trade up in Round 1 -- and six should trade down
content: the Jets trade up in the first round to select Ohio State's Carnell Tate . a draft-day trade is a thought exercise based on what teams are likely to be thinking about . this column is about what organizations should do with their picks at the end of the month .

title: Grading all 30 NBA teams' regular seasons
content: the 2026 champion has been an eventful 2025-26, full of superlative performances, shocking trades and surprises up and down the league standings . here, we hand out regular season grades to all 30 teams using a simple rubric: Did a team meet, exceed or fall short of its preseason expectations?

title: Postseason roundtable: NHL player, coach, GM predict the 2026 Stanley Cup playoffs
content: we decided to apply that template to the Stanley Cup playoffs to glean insight from those on the inside . here are a veteran Eastern Conference player, a Western Conference coach and an NHL general manager with their outlooks on the postseason . they'll beat whoever they play in the first round .

title: The legacy of Jackie Robinson, baseball's greatest agent of change
content: baseball was his weakest sport . he was a much better basketball, football and track athlete than a baseball player . some say he's an even better tennis player than the greece Dodgers . "were there better baseball players in the Negro Leagues than Jackie?" he said .
